step_by_step_thinking:
The numbness experienced by the patient after parotidectomy suggests a disruption in the pathway of the auriculotemporal nerve since this nerve innervates areas surrounding the parotid gland, notably the external auditory meatus and the part of the earlobe. Given that the procedure often necessitates sacrificing the greater auricular nerve, it could directly contribute to numbness in the lobule area, yet considering the typical presentation of Frey syndrome – characterized by hypersensitivity and sweating upon stimulation — it aligns closely with the condition induced primarily by auriculotemporal nerve compromise.
Hence, I conclude that the correct answer is \boxed{B}.

Given that the procedure often necessitates sacrificing the greater auricular nerve, it could directly contribute to numbness in the lobule area, yet considering the typical presentation of Frey syndrome – characterized by hypersensitivity and sweating upon stimulation — it aligns closely with the condition induced primarily by auriculotemporal nerve compromise.
Hence, I conclude that the correct answer is \boxed{B}.
